name: kiln-ai
documentation:
# Kiln AI Core Library

[![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/kiln-ai.svg?logo=pypi&label=PyPI&logoColor=gold)](https://pypi.org/project/kiln-ai) [![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/kiln-ai.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/kiln-ai) [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-pdoc-blue)](https://kiln-ai.github.io/Kiln/kiln_core_docs/index.html) --- ## Installation ```console pip install kiln_ai ``` ## About This package is the Kiln AI core library. There is also a separate desktop application and server package. Learn more about Kiln AI at [getkiln.ai](https://getkiln.ai) - Github: [github.com/Kiln-AI/kiln](https://github.com/Kiln-AI/kiln) - Core Library Docs: [https://kiln-ai.github.io/Kiln/kiln_core_docs/index.html](https://kiln-ai.github.io/Kiln/kiln_core_docs/index.html) ## Quick Start ```python from kiln_ai.datamodel import Project print("Reading Kiln project") project = Project.load_from_file("path/to/project.kiln") print("Project: ", project.name, " - ", project.description) task = project.tasks()[0] print("Task: ", task.name, " - ", task.description) print("Total dataset size:", len(task.runs())) # ... app specific code using the typed kiln datamodel # Alternatively, load data into pandas or a similar tool: import glob import json import pandas as pd from pathlib import Path dataitem_glob = str(task.path.parent) + "/runs/*/task_run.kiln" dfs = [] for file in glob.glob(dataitem_glob): js = json.loads(Path(file).read_text()) df = pd.json_normalize(js) dfs.append(df) final_df = pd.concat(dfs, ignore_index=True) print(final_df) ```

documentation: schema_version

Base model for all Kiln data models with common functionality for persistence and versioning.

Attributes:
    v (int): Schema version number for migration support
    id (str): Unique identifier for the model instance
    path (Path): File system path where the model is stored
    created_at (datetime): Timestamp when the model was created
    created_by (str): User ID of the creator

class_variables.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.basemodel.KilnParentedModel.parent
documentation: type: ignore

Base model for Kiln models that have a parent-child relationship. This base class is for child models.

This class provides functionality for managing hierarchical relationships between models,
including parent reference handling and file system organization.

Attributes:
    _parent (KilnBaseModel): Reference to the parent model instance

classes.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.basemodel.KilnParentModel

methods.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.basemodel.KilnParentModel.validate_and_save_with_subrelations
documentation:
Validate and save a model instance along with all its nested child relationships.

Args:
    data (Dict[str, Any]): Model data including child relationships
    path (Path, optional): Path where the model should be saved
    parent (KilnBaseModel, optional): Parent model instance for parented models

Returns:
    KilnParentModel: The validated and saved model instance

Raises:
    ValidationError: If validation fails for the model or any of its children

Base model for Kiln models that can have child models.

This class provides functionality for managing collections of child models and their persistence.
Child relationships must be defined using the parent_of parameter in the class definition.

Args:
    parent_of (Dict[str, Type[KilnParentedModel]]): Mapping of relationship names to child model types
Parent create methods for all child relationships
You must pass in parent_of in the subclass definition, defining the child relationships

A rating for a task output, including an overall rating and ratings for each requirement.

Only supports five star ratings for now, but extensible for custom values.

An output for a specific task run.

Contains the actual output content, its source (human or synthetic),

documentation:
The source type of a piece of data.

Human: a human created the data
Synthetic: a model created the data

classes.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.DataSourceProperty
documentation:
Defines a property that can be associated with a data source.

Includes validation rules for when properties are required or not allowed
based on the data source type.

classes.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.DataSource
documentation:
Represents the origin of data, either human or synthetic, with associated properties.

Properties vary based on the source type - for synthetic sources this includes
model information, for human sources this includes creator information.

classes.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.TaskRun
documentation:
Represents a single execution of a Task.

Contains the input used, its source, the output produced, and optional
repair information if the output needed correction.

A definition of a split in a dataset.

Example: name="train", description="The training set", percentage=0.8 (80% of the dataset)

methods.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.DatasetSplit.missing_count
documentation:
Returns:
    int: the number of task runs that have an ID persisted in this dataset split, but no longer exist in the dataset

A collection of task runs, with optional splits (train, test, validation).

Used to freeze a dataset into train/test/validation splits for repeatable fine-tuning or other tasks.

Maintains a list of IDs for each split, to avoid data duplication.

classes.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.TaskRequirement
documentation:
Defines a specific requirement that should be met by task outputs.

Includes an identifier, name, description, instruction for meeting the requirement,
and priority level.

classes.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.TaskDeterminism

class_variables.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.TaskDeterminism.deterministic
documentation: Expect exact match

class_variables.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.TaskDeterminism.semantic_match
documentation: Expect same meaning, but flexible on expression of the meaning

class_variables.name: kiln_ai.datamodel.TaskDeterminism.flexible
documentation: Flexible on semantic output. Eval should be custom based on parsing requirements.

Defines how strictly task outputs should match expected results.

- deterministic: Requires exact matches
- semantic_match: Allows different wording with same meaning
- flexible: Allows variation in both wording and meaning within requirements

classes.name: kiln_ai.adapters.base_adapter.BaseAdapter

methods.name: kiln_ai.adapters.base_adapter.BaseAdapter.generate_run
documentation: create a run and task output

Base class for AI model adapters that handle task execution.

This abstract class provides the foundation for implementing model-specific adapters
that can process tasks with structured or unstructured inputs/outputs. It handles
input/output validation, prompt building, and run tracking.

Attributes:
    prompt_builder (BasePromptBuilder): Builder for constructing prompts for the model
    kiln_task (Task): The task configuration and metadata
    output_schema (dict | None): JSON schema for validating structured outputs
    input_schema (dict | None): JSON schema for validating structured inputs

modules.name: kiln_ai.adapters
documentation:
# Adapters

Adapters are used to connect Kiln to external systems, or to add new functionality to Kiln.

BaseAdapter is extensible, and used for adding adapters that provide AI functionality. There's currently a LangChain adapter which provides a bridge to LangChain.

The ml_model_list submodule contains a list of models that can be used for machine learning tasks. More can easily be added, but we keep a list here of models that are known to work well with Kiln's structured data and tool calling systems.

The prompt_builders submodule contains classes that build prompts for use with the AI agents.

The repair submodule contains an adapter for the repair task.

modules.name: kiln_ai.adapters.data_gen.data_gen_prompts
documentation: The contents of this file are adapted from the promptwrite library (https://github.com/StacklokLabs/promptwright),
which was adapted from the pluto library (https://github.com/redotvideo/pluto).
These libraries are licensed under the Apache License 2.0. Any modifications
are licensed under the kiln AI Core license (MIT at time of writing). See /libs/core/LICENSE.txt for details.

modules.name: kiln_ai.adapters.data_gen
documentation:
# Data Generation

A task to generate synthetic data for Kiln Tasks. This generates the inputs, which then can be run through the task.

Optional human guidance can be provided to guide the generation process.

classes.name: kiln_ai.adapters.ml_model_list.ModelName
documentation:
Enumeration of specific model versions supported by the system.
Where models have instruct and raw versions, instruct is default and raw is specified.
Where models have instruct and raw versions, instruct is default and raw is specified

classes.name: kiln_ai.adapters.ml_model_list.KilnModelProvider
documentation:
Configuration for a specific model provider.

Attributes:
    name: The provider's identifier
    supports_structured_output: Whether the provider supports structured output formats
    supports_data_gen: Whether the provider supports data generation
    untested_model: Whether the model is untested (typically user added). The supports_ fields are not applicable.
    provider_finetune_id: The finetune ID for the provider, if applicable
    provider_options: Additional provider-specific configuration options
    adapter_options: Additional options specific to the adapter. Top level key should be adapter ID.

classes.name: kiln_ai.adapters.ml_model_list.KilnModel
documentation:
Configuration for a specific AI model.

Attributes:
    family: The model's architecture family
    name: The model's identifier
    friendly_name: Human-readable name for the model
    providers: List of providers that offer this model
    supports_structured_output: Whether the model supports structured output formats

Input model for generating categories/subtopics.

Attributes:

    system_prompt: System prompt to guide the AI generation
    num_subtopics: Number of subtopics to generate
    human_guidance: Optional human guidance to influence generation
    existing_topics: Optional list of existing topics to avoid duplication

Convert a name used in the UI to the corresponding prompt builder class.

methods.name: kiln_ai.adapters.prompt_builders.BasePromptBuilder.prompt_builder_name
documentation:
Returns the name of the prompt builder, to be used for persisting into the datastore.

Default implementation gets the name of the prompt builder in snake case. If you change the class name, you should override this so prior saved data is compatible.

methods.name: kiln_ai.adapters.fine_tune.dataset_formatter.DatasetFormatter.dump_to_file
documentation:
Format the dataset into the specified format.

Args:
    split_name: Name of the split to dump
    format_type: Format to generate the dataset in
    path: Optional path to write to. If None, writes to temp directory

Returns:
    Path to the generated file
Handles formatting of datasets into various output formats

functions.name: kiln_ai.utils.name_generator.generate_memorable_name
documentation:
Generates a memorable two-word name combining a random adjective and noun.

Returns:
    str: A memorable name in the format "Adjective Noun"

Example:
    >>> generate_memorable_name()
    'Cosmic Dragon'
